cases.prompt: set up pre-commit for this repo
add pre-commit hooks with ruff and mypy to this python project
pre-commit passes locally but ruff fails in CI on the same files, why?
enforce conventional commit messages with a git hook
can u add somthing that formats the yaml and markdown files before commit
the ruff hook auto-fixes files but the commit still goes through - make it block
run mypy and pytest only on push, not on every commit
should we switch from pre-commit to prek?
devs keep skipping the hooks with --no-verify, how do we make the checks stick
validate pyproject.toml and the github workflow files before every commit
mirrors-prettier is archived, what should our pre-commit config use now
block commits with trailing whitespace and leftover merge conflict markers
set up ruff and pick which lint rule sets to enable in pyproject.toml
add a Claude Code hook that runs ruff after every file edit
set up a GitHub Actions workflow that runs lint, mypy and pytest on every PR
fix these mypy errors in the parser module
set up dependabot to keep our dependencies updated
cut a release - bump the version and generate the changelog from the conventional commits
write an AGENTS.md so coding agents stop bypassing our checks
make the lint and test checks required before merging to main
enable secret scanning and push protection on the github repo
undo my last commit but keep the changes staged
Set up pre-commit for this Python project
expected_behavior: checks the repo first (existing config, ruff/mypy versions in dev dependencies, whether GitHub workflows exist) and pins a rev for every hook repo it writes
includes hygiene hooks from pre-commit/pre-commit-hooks plus ruff-check with --fix and --exit-non-zero-on-fix ordered before ruff-format
runs pre-commit run --all-files after installing and gets the existing tree clean rather than leaving hooks that fail on untouched files
states that hooks are bypassable with --no-verify and wires or recommends the CI mirror (pre-commit run --all-files in CI or pre-commit.ci)
gives install commands that cover every stage used (default_install_hook_types or pre-commit install --hook-type)

cases.prompt: the ruff pre-commit hook auto-fixed my files but the commit went through anyway - I want it to block so the fix gets reviewed
expected_behavior: adds --exit-non-zero-on-fix to the ruff hook args so the hook fails whenever it applies a fix
explains that fixes are left unstaged and the fail, re-add, re-commit loop is expected pre-commit behavior
keeps the lint/fix hook ordered before ruff-format
does not touch ruff rule selection in pyproject.toml

cases.prompt: add formatting and validation for the yaml, markdown and toml files in this python repo
expected_behavior: scopes the Markdown/YAML/JSON formatter with types_or so it never touches Python files
uses the maintained rbubley/mirrors-prettier fork (not the archived pre-commit/mirrors-prettier) or offers the Node-free stack (mdformat, taplo, yamllint) with the trade-off stated
pairs formatters with validators (validate-pyproject, check-jsonschema for GitHub workflows) because formatting does not catch structural errors
pins a rev for every hook repo added

cases.prompt: pre-commit takes about 30 seconds so everyone here commits with --no-verify, fix it
expected_behavior: profiles the hooks (pre-commit run --verbose or equivalent) to find the slow ones instead of guessing
moves slow checks like mypy or tests to the pre-push stage or CI, keeping the commit stage to a few seconds
is honest that --no-verify cannot be blocked locally and confirms the same checks run in CI as the real gate
mentions SKIP=<hook-id> as the narrower escape hatch instead of blanket --no-verify

cases.prompt: pre-commit passes on my machine but the ruff job in CI fails on the same files
expected_behavior: diagnoses version skew between the ruff-pre-commit rev and the ruff version installed in CI or the dev dependency group
aligns the versions and proposes a sync mechanism (Dependabot pre-commit ecosystem, pre-commit.ci autoupdate, or a scheduled reviewed autoupdate)
recommends CI run the identical config via pre-commit run --all-files rather than invoking the tools separately
